segments.integrated_caption: The 'Ellada Exei Talento' stage is brightly lit in blue, showcasing the judges' panel with prominent 'X' logos and a lively audience. Upbeat, rhythmic background music plays as a large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, identified by on-screen text as 'ΤΑΣΟΣ ΓΕΩΡΓΙΟΥ', walks onto the stage. He greets the hosts, introducing himself as Tasos Georgiou, and confirms he is from Athens, specifically Kypseli. He engages in a conversation with the hosts, revealing he will perform Karagiozis (shadow theater) and has been involved professionally since sixth grade, now being 32 years old. The judges, including a male judge in a grey checkered blazer, listen intently, nodding and showing amusement. The audience claps and reacts positively. Behind Tasos, a colorful, hand-painted backdrop depicting a whimsical parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks is visible, later subtly changing to a castle silhouette. As the hosts wish him well, the background music transitions to a distinct Karagiozis-style melody, and Tasos begins his act with high-pitched, theatrical vocalizations, prompting laughter and continued positive reactions from the audience and judges, with one male judge covering his mouth in amusement.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, clapping and reacting with visible enthusiasm, their applause and cheering audible as lively, traditional Greek-sounding music begins. A male voice exclaims "Opa!" before the scene transitions to a shadow puppet performance. Silhouetted, cartoonish figures move dynamically across a brightly lit screen, set against a hand-painted backdrop initially depicting a tree and a castle, suggesting a fairy-tale setting. The male voice continues with rhythmic, chant-like speech, including phrases like "Opa, opa, opa, opa, hey!" and "Για ρε γλεντια!" As the shadow figures dance and interact, with one large figure appearing to lead, the camera cuts to the judges’ panel. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer is seen laughing and covering his mouth in amusement, while the Female Judge with Platinum Hair observes with a composed, attentive expression. The performance maintains a lively, whimsical tone, with the shadow figures engaging in synchronized movements, accompanied by the male voice delivering theatrical lines such as "Ε ρε να μου ζήσεις ξυπόλητο τάγμα!" and the repeated, comedic "Μερσί Μαέστρο!" (Merci Maestro!). The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt is also shown reacting with amusement. The male voice continues with playful, self-referential dialogue, mentioning "Μερσί Μαέστρο μου, μερσί και η μύτη σου τουρσί" (Merci Maestro, merci and your nose is pickled). The backdrop is now clearly visible as a colorful, hand-painted scene featuring a parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks, adding to the narrative depth. The male voice then introduces the act: "Πολυτήρης Καλατζόπουλος, παρών! Τι παράσταση θα παρουσιάσουμε απόψε πουλάκι μου; Αξιότιμες κυρίες και κύριοι, αγαπητοί μικροί μας φίλοι, γεια σας και χαρά σας." (Polytiris Kalantzopoulos, present! What performance will we present tonight, my little bird? Esteemed ladies and gentlemen, dear young friends, hello and joy to you.) He concludes by announcing, "Απόψε θα σας διασκεδάσουμε με μία ωραία παράσταση ο πατέρας μου στο Ελλάδα έχεις ταλέντο" (Tonight we will entertain you with a beautiful performance by my father on Greece Got Talent), as the on-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' remains visible. The segment concludes with the shadow puppets continuing their dance, the judges still engaged, and the audience’s positive energy palpable, with intensified applause and cheering as the male voice says, "Συγχαρητήρια! Περάστε τώρα γρήγορα." (Congratulations! Come quickly now.)

The audio reveals a comedic dialogue between two distinct male voices, identified as the traditional Greek shadow puppet characters Hadjiavatis and Karagiozis. Hadjiavatis, speaking with a deeper, more authoritative voice, calls out to Karagiozis, who responds with a higher-pitched, comedic voice, leading to bursts of audience laughter and applause. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, rhythmic musical score as the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, seated at the judges' panel, reacts with amusement, at one point covering his mouth. The scene quickly transitions to a wide shot of the stage, revealing the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer and the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, both engaged and smiling, while the audience claps and reacts positively under blue stage lighting. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer asks, "Παίξε μας και κάτι, ε;" (Play something for us, eh?), and then greets Tasos Georgiou, "Γεια σου κιόλας, Τάσο." (Hello there, Tasos.), to which Tasos replies, "Ευχαριστώ πολύ." (Thank you very much.). The judge then addresses the Assistant Performer, "Γιώργο, παίξε μας και κάτι." (George, play something for us.), and asks, "Πώς είσαι;" (How are you?), as Tasos responds, "Ωραίο θα 'ναι." (It will be nice.). The focus shifts to Tasos Georgiou, a large man in a black button-up shirt, interacting with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, who stands up and approaches the performance area. Tasos holds a shadow puppet rod, and together with the Assistant Performer, they manipulate shadow puppets on a white screen. The backdrop features a hand-painted castle and later transitions to a parade scene with cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer identifies one puppet, stating, "Αυτό λοιπόν είναι ο Κολιτήρης." (So this is Kolitiris.), which Tasos confirms, "Ναι, ο Κολιτήρης." (Yes, Kolitiris.). The judge explains the puppet's movement, "Τον βάζουμε και να πατήσει κάτω, δέβαια." (We also make him step down, of course.), and Tasos acknowledges, "Α, πατάει εδώ, ναι." (Ah, he steps here, yes.), followed by the judge's instruction, "Ακριβώς. Τον ακουμπάμε στον πανί και τον κουνάμε." (Exactly. We touch him to the screen and move him.). The judge praises their effort, "Είναι μια πολύ καλή προσπάθεια για πρώτη φορά." (It's a very good effort for a first time.), to which Tasos responds with a surprised "Αλήθεια;" (Really?) and a laugh. The judge then urges, "Πες κάτι, Γιώργο, πες κάτι!" (Say something, George, say something!), and after a brief, unclear response from George, Tasos exclaims, "Κοίτα, εγώ αυτό που είδα μ' άρεσε πολύ. Λέω να πω... Ναι!" (Look, I really liked what I saw. I'm going to say... Yes!), prompting loud cheers and applause from the audience. The judge then asks, "Αυτό τι είναι;" (What is this?), and explains, "Αυτό λοιπόν είναι ο Ισπαλιάρας που ρίχνει ο Καραγκιόζης στον Χατζηαβάτη." (So this is the 'Ispaliaras' that Karagiozis throws at Hatziavatis.), with Tasos asking for clarification, "Πώς γίνεται αυτό;" (How does this happen?). The judge suggests, "Ε, μπορούμε να το δείξουμε." (Uh, we can show it.), adding, "Μπορεί να το δείξει και ο βοηθός μας, εφόσον ήτανε κι αυτός μαζί μας." (Our assistant can also show it, since he was with us too.), which Tasos affirms, "Ναι, ναι, ναι." (Yes, yes, yes.). The shadow puppets, including one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs and another in a blue and gold costume with red boots, perform animated gestures, suggesting a narrative dialogue or confrontation, as the judge concludes by naming another puppet, "Καραγκιόζης." The judges and audience continue to react with amusement and applause, with dynamic spotlights highlighting the stage and performers, creating a vibrant and engaging atmosphere throughout the performance.
